title: phaca frigida var. lapponica
intro: Phaca frigida var. lapponica, also known as the Arctic lupine, is a flowering plant species found in the Arctic tundra regions. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Phaca frigida var. lapponica include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparation of this plant is typically in the form of infusions or decoctions, where the leaves and flowers are steeped in hot water to create a medicinal tea.
